node.statement: (GNS for density operators on B(H)). For a normal state φ_ρ with ρ of finite rank r, the GNS representation π_ρ is unitarily equivalent to id^{⊕r} (r copies of the identity representation of B(H) on H). The commutant is π_ρ(B(H))' ≅ M_r(C), so dim_R(commutant^{sa}) = r². For rank-∞ density operators (e.g., thermal states), the commutant is infinite-dimensional.
